content: First-line treatment for MDD includes SSRIs (e.g., sertraline, escitalopram) or SNRIs (e.g., venlafaxine, duloxetine), often combined with psychotherapy (CBT or IPT). Treatment selection considers symptom profile, comorbidities, side effect tolerance, and patient preference. For inadequate response after 4-8 weeks, options include dose optimization, switching antidepressants, or augmentation (e.g., bupropion, mirtazapine, lithium, or atypical antipsychotics like aripiprazole). Severe or treatment-resistant cases may benefit from ECT, rTMS, or ketamine/esketamine. Maintenance therapy for 6-12 months after remission reduces relapse risk; longer duration is recommended for recurrent depression.
